headline: Real-time voice AI tooling broke out as collision detection and audio processing surged to the top of the rotation.
body: The TEN Framework drove a sharp move in conversational voice AI, an open-source stack for interruptible agents that handles VAD, turn detection, collision detection, and real-time audio pipelines beyond the typical STT-LLM-TTS chain. On the technique side, brand voice, post-generation filtering, and knowledge indexing entered as new, with a four-layer brand voice architecture replacing flat tone instructions to prevent prompt degradation over long conversations. A summit of OpenAI, Anthropic, and Box leaders centered on the federal recall of Anthropic's Fable and Mythos models, infrastructure buildout viability, and a shift from token optimization to outcome optimization. Claude tooling also moved, with a roundup of 17 plugins spanning RAG, web scraping, and automation integrations.

cited_items.title: TEN Framework: Voice AI That Can Actually Be Interrupted
